edges.description: Germline cells are highly sensitive to MCM8-dependent genome-maintenance failure: unresolved meiotic recombination intermediates block progression through prophase I.
edges.target: Meiotic arrest and germ cell depletion

edges.description: In 46,XX individuals, germ-cell depletion exhausts the ovarian follicle pool and produces primary ovarian insufficiency.
edges.source: Meiotic arrest and germ cell depletion
edges.target: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: In 46,XY individuals, the same germ-cell depletion produces spermatogenic failure presenting as non-obstructive azoospermia.
edges.source: Meiotic arrest and germ cell depletion

edges.description: The female branch presents clinically as primary / premature ovarian insufficiency.
edges.source: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: In 46,XY individuals severe spermatogenic failure manifests clinically as non-obstructive azoospermia.
The somatic instability state drives an early-onset neoplasia phenotype, especially gonadal germ cell tumors.
edges.source: Somatic genome instability and germ cell tumor predisposition

nodes.description: In 46,XY individuals the disorder manifests as severe non-obstructive azoospermia, frequently with small testes.
Biallelic MCM8 variant carriers show an association with early-onset germ cell tumors, a narrower cancer spectrum than the gastrointestinal malignancy reported for MCM9.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical syndromes linked to biallelic germline variants in MCM8 and MCM9.
evidence.snippet: both biallelic MCM8 and MCM9 variants are linked to hypogonadism and the early development of germ cell tumors.

nodes.description: Biallelic MCM8 loss disables the MCM8-MCM9 helicase, reducing RAD51 recruitment and homologous-recombination repair of DNA double-strand breaks. This genome-maintenance defect is especially damaging during meiosis, where programmed double-strand breaks must be repaired by homologous recombination.

biological_processes: double-strand break repair via homologous recombination

evidence.reference_title: The MCM8-MCM9 complex promotes RAD51 recruitment at DNA damage sites to facilitate homologous recombination.
evidence.snippet: Consistent with a role in the repair of ICLs by homologous recombination (HR), knockdown of MCM8 or MCM9 significantly reduces HR repair efficiency.

evidence.reference_title: The MCM8-MCM9 complex promotes RAD51 recruitment at DNA damage sites to facilitate homologous recombination.
evidence.snippet: Chromatin immunoprecipitation analysis using human DR-GFP cells or Xenopus egg extract demonstrated that MCM8 and MCM9 proteins are rapidly recruited to DNA damage sites and promote RAD51 recruitment.

evidence.reference_title: MCM8- and MCM9-deficient mice reveal gametogenesis defects and genome instability due to impaired homologous recombination.
evidence.snippet: chromatin recruitment of HR factors like Rad51 and RPA is impaired and HR strongly reduced.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing reveals MCM8 mutation underlies ovarian failure and chromosomal instability.
evidence.snippet: SNP analysis and whole-exome sequencing revealed the presence of a pathogenic variant of the minichromosome maintenance 8 gene (MCM8, c.446C>G; p.P149R) located within a region of homozygosity

evidence.reference_title: A novel homozygous frameshift mutation in MCM8 causes primary gonadal dysgenesis in both genders.
evidence.snippet: A homozygous frameshift mutation c.998delG (p. Gly333Glufs*50) in MCM8 was identified in the two siblings.

nodes.description: Germ cells that cannot complete homologous recombination arrest during meiotic prophase I and are eliminated rather than maturing into functional gametes, depleting the germ-cell pool in both sexes.
nodes.id: Meiotic arrest and germ cell depletion

evidence.reference_title: MCM8- and MCM9-deficient mice reveal gametogenesis defects and genome instability due to impaired homologous recombination.
evidence.snippet: MCM8(-/-) mice are sterile because spermatocytes are blocked in meiotic prophase I, and females have only arrested primary follicles and frequently develop ovarian tumors.

nodes.description: Loss of oocytes during meiotic progression depletes the ovarian reserve, presenting clinically as primary ovarian insufficiency, ovarian dysgenesis, primary amenorrhea, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism in 46,XX individuals.
nodes.id: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing reveals MCM8 mutation underlies ovarian failure and chromosomal instability.
evidence.snippet: Here, we studied 3 sisters with primary amenorrhea, hypothyroidism, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.

nodes.description: Reported 46,XX individuals present with primary ovarian insufficiency or ovarian dysgenesis, often with primary amenorrhea and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing reveals MCM8 mutation underlies ovarian failure and chromosomal instability.
evidence.snippet: Here, we studied 3 sisters with primary amenorrhea, hypothyroidism, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.

nodes.description: Beyond the gonads, MCM8-dependent homologous-recombination failure produces a somatic genome-instability state. In humans this is associated with an emerging predisposition to early-onset germ cell tumors; murine Mcm8 loss likewise produces ovarian tumors. This cancer profile is narrower than the gastrointestinal polyposis / colorectal-cancer spectrum reported for MCM9.
nodes.id: Somatic genome instability and germ cell tumor predisposition

biological_processes: double-strand break repair via homologous recombination

evidence.reference_title: Clinical syndromes linked to biallelic germline variants in MCM8 and MCM9.
evidence.snippet: both biallelic MCM8 and MCM9 variants are linked to hypogonadism and the early development of germ cell tumors.

evidence.reference_title: MCM8- and MCM9-deficient mice reveal gametogenesis defects and genome instability due to impaired homologous recombination.
evidence.snippet: MCM8(-/-) mice are sterile because spermatocytes are blocked in meiotic prophase I, and females have only arrested primary follicles and frequently develop ovarian tumors.

nodes.description: In 46,XY individuals, MCM8-dependent meiotic failure in spermatocytes produces spermatogenic arrest with small testes, non-obstructive azoospermia, and Sertoli cell-only histology.

evidence.reference_title: Minichromosome maintenance complex component 8 (MCM8) gene mutations result in primary gonadal failure.
evidence.snippet: two consanguineous families presenting as primary amenorrhoea in the females and as small testes and azoospermia in a male.

evidence.reference_title: A novel homozygous frameshift mutation in MCM8 causes primary gonadal dysgenesis in both genders.
evidence.snippet: The testes tissue sections in the male showed a Sertoli cell-only syndrome (SCOS).
